historical_lineage: Section 7 Claude/UCP workforce experience plus m365-enterprise-readiness-master-plan
objective: Repair the live activation path so a user-approved Claude or Codex MCP session can activate truthfully, admit governed tools consistently, and execute real M365 actions without collapsing into contradictory policy_pack, session_not_activated, or fake-credentials failure states.
decision_rule: ActivationPathHealthy = PackEnabled AND ActivationAllowed AND SessionStateReachable. LiveToolPathHealthy = ActivationPathHealthy AND ToolRegistrationConsistent AND PolicyAdmissionConsistent. LiveM365Callable = LiveToolPathHealthy AND TenantContextReachable AND SitesRootGreen AND (DirectoryOrgGreen OR RealGraphPermissionError). RepairReady = LiveM365Callable.
prompt_discipline: Maintain a formal prompt pair under docs/prompts/ following the repo two-file prompt rule and the MATHS prompt template. The M365 repo is the planning authority; the sibling UCP repo is the implementation target for the runtime repair.

intent_definition.build: Repair the live Claude -> UCP -> M365 control plane so activation, governance admission, and tool execution agree on the same session state.
intent_definition.why: The current runtime says M365 tools exist while blocking activate_session, which cascades into session_not_activated for real M365 calls.
intent_definition.problem: Remove the broken state machine where discovery says available, policy says forbidden, and execution says not activated.
boundaries: Repair activation and tool-surface truthfulness only.
Do not loosen Microsoft tenant permissions or relabel downstream Graph authorization failures as connector bugs.
guarantees: User-approved activation succeeds or fails with the true blocking reason.
Stdio and HTTP surfaces do not disagree about callable M365 reality.
sites.root executes live once activation is green.
directory.org either succeeds or fails with a real Graph permission error, never credentials_missing or session_not_activated after activation.
success_criteria: activate_session(confirm=true) succeeds on the live path.
validate_action and constraint_status remain callable after activation.
m365_action agent=m365-administrator action=directory.org reaches real Graph auth and returns either success or a real Graph-side permission error.
intent_definition.determinism: For fixed runtime config, license, tenant contract, and client identity, repeated activation and first-hop M365 calls produce the same admission decisions and outcome class.

observed_failure: The live UCP server reports m365_pack enabled.
Fresh MCP stdio sessions list m365_action and m365_sites.
The same sessions reject activate_session because policy_pack says the tool is not allowed.
Because activation is blocked, downstream M365 calls fail with session_not_activated.
The generic HTTP tool route does not currently expose validate_action and has shown parity gaps for M365 tool IDs.

repair_order.description: Freeze the failure signatures and lock deterministic success criteria.
Repair policy admission for activation bypass tools.
Repair or document stdio and HTTP tool-surface parity.
Reprove activation, session_status, validate_action, and constraint_status.
Reprove sites.root and directory.org on the live runtime.
Synchronize governance and close the slice truthfully.

non_goals: Do not reopen already-fixed tenant-context work unless regression evidence proves it.
Do not add non-M365 channels or alternate auth stories.
Do not claim live parity is green before retained evidence exists.

in: Capture the exact live failure baseline for activation, session-state, and first-hop M365 calls.
Repair activation admission in the sibling UCP runtime.
Repair or document stdio and HTTP tool-surface parity.
Reprove activate_session, session_status, validate_action, sites.root, and directory.org.
Synchronize M365 governance docs and prompt artifacts.
out: Changing Microsoft Graph tenant permissions except to classify downstream failures truthfully.
Reopening already-fixed tenant-context work without regression evidence.
Claiming live parity is green before retained evidence exists.

phase_stack.title: Failure baseline and intent lock
phase_stack.goal: Freeze the observed live failure signatures, exact boundaries, and deterministic success criteria before implementation.
outputs: retained activation and session-state error signatures

children.title: Baseline capture and repair intent lock
children.goal: Record the exact policy_pack activation rejection and session_not_activated cascade as the authoritative starting state.

phase_stack.title: Activation admission repair
phase_stack.goal: Repair the live activation path so user-approved session activation is admitted consistently through the actual runtime.

children.title: Policy-pack admission repair
children.goal: Fix the policy and allowlist path so activation bypass tools are not contradicted by pack-level admission.

children.title: Session-state projection repair
children.goal: Ensure activation state is visible and consistent to governed tools immediately after activation.

phase_stack.title: Tool-surface parity repair
phase_stack.goal: Ensure stdio and HTTP surfaces project the same callable reality for the M365 runtime.

children.title: HTTP and stdio parity
children.goal: Remove or document the mismatch where the pack is enabled but some live tool surfaces do not expose the same callable IDs.

phase_stack.title: Live M365 reproof
phase_stack.goal: Re-run the live first-hop M365 proofs after activation and parity are repaired.

children.title: Live activation and governance reproof
children.goal: Reprove activate_session, session_status, constraint_status, and validate_action on the repaired runtime.

children.title: Live M365 first-hop reproof
children.goal: Reprove m365_sites sites.root and m365_action directory.org, classifying any remaining failure as real downstream Microsoft auth and permission behavior.

phase_stack.title: Governance closeout
phase_stack.goal: Synchronize the retained evidence and final status across M365 and UCP governance surfaces.
outputs: prompt artifacts aligned to retained evidence

children.title: Repair closeout and operator handoff
children.goal: Close the repair slice with a truthful live-state summary and the next required action, if any.
